Add R586-R603: telehealth, PNE, ACT, vestibular rehab, cryotherapy protocol, pregnancy complications, feeding, Mediterranean diet, and more

Integrated 15 research findings across 16 existing pages:
- R586: Telehealth for rural specialty access (insurance-navigation)
- R587: Pain Neuroscience Education dose-response (pain-management)
- R588: Kinesiology taping for proprioception (orthotics-bracing)
- R589: Hearing loss critical period for language (hearing-management)
- R590: Pediatric palliative care for non-malignant conditions (transition-adult-care)
- R591: Muscle strengthening protocols for hypermobility (hypermobility-management)
- R592: Dry needling and trigger point therapy (complementary-therapies)
- R593: Feeding difficulties in craniofacial involvement (pierre-robin)
- R594: Anti-inflammatory Mediterranean diet gut-joint-disc triad (diet-protocol)
- R595: Genetic counseling psychological impact (genetic-counseling)
- R596: Compression garments for POTS (autonomic-dysfunction)
- R597: Spondylolisthesis in CTD children (lumbar-stenosis)
- R598: Wound healing and collagen supplements (wound-healing)
- R599: ACT mediator analysis for pediatric chronic pain (psychological-support)
- R600: Vestibular rehabilitation for SNHL (hearing-management, vestibular-dysfunction)
- R601: Pregnancy complications in CTD (pregnancy-management)
- R602: Wobble board proprioception protocol (proprioception-balance)
- R603: Cambridge Protocol implementation guide (eye-management)

commit.message:
Fix PR selection logic to prefer open and recently updated PRs

When multiple PRs share the same commit SHA, the workflow now intelligently
selects the most relevant PR instead of always picking the first one (lowest
number).

New selection priority:
1. Open PRs over closed/merged PRs
2. Most recently updated PR

This fixes ops workflow deployments showing PR #1 instead of the actual PR
where the deployment was triggered (e.g., PR #10).

Files changed:
- .github/workflows/ops.yml: Updated PR lookup in deployment creation
- .github/scripts/update-deployment-wiki.js: Updated PR lookup in wiki generation

commit.message:
fix: robust invitation acceptance - retry logic, session wait, diagnostic logs

- register.vue waits 1s for session then retries accept-invitation 3x
- index.vue uses stored inviteId as fallback, retries workspace fetch 3x
- accept-invitation logs user resolution path for debugging
- Invited users reliably land in dashboard, not onboarding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse industry_taxonomy JSONB as object (not string)

asyncpg returns JSONB as a string by default; added json.loads
on the backend and defensive JSON.parse on the frontend to prevent
Object.entries from iterating over characters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rename get_session endpoint to avoid shadowing DB dependency

The endpoint handler for GET /sessions/{session_id} was named
get_session, which shadowed the get_session database dependency imported
from database.py. All subsequent endpoints using Depends(get_session)
were injecting the route handler instead of an AsyncSession, causing
AttributeError on all checklist write operations (toggle, complete,
add items, add templates, shares).

Also fix: expire cached session object before reloading after commit in
add_templates_to_session, so selectinload fetches newly added items.

commit.message:
v0.1.2: Start reconnect watchdog at serve() startup

Previously _schedule_reconnect_check() was only called from _on_datagram(),
so if the wallbox was already silent at HA startup (e.g. after an HA restart),
the watchdog never started and no wakeup broadcast was ever sent.

Now serve() initialises last_message_time and schedules the first check
immediately after binding the UDP socket. After message_timeout seconds
without a datagram the wakeup broadcast is sent automatically.

commit.message:
feat: Digest → Recap (platform-wide synthesis, Pass 2)

Display rename: Digest → Recap across UI, landing page, type labels.
Behavioral change: platform-wide scope (all synced sources for a
platform) instead of single channel/label/page.

- Card: "Recap" / "Catch up on a connected platform — daily or weekly"
- Skill: asks platform + frequency, auto-populates all sources,
  1 recap per platform per user duplicate guard
- Prompt v2: Highlights (top 3-5) + By Source (subsection per
  channel/label/page) with platform-specific grouping hints
- Internal type key remains `digest`

commit.message:
Fase 8.E: optimizar BlitRenderTextureToDDraw (~45% menos operaciones)

3 optimizaciones combinadas en el loop de blit SFML->DDraw:

1. Alpha sentinel: clear con alpha=0 en vez de RGB(0,1,0).
   Check por pixel: 3 comparaciones de bytes -> 1 mask (rgba & 0xFF000000).
   Seguro porque todos los sprites usan alpha=255 (opaco) o alpha=0 (colorkey).

2. uint32 reads: leer RGBA como 1 dword en vez de 3 bytes separados.
   En x86 little-endian: uint32 = A<<24|B<<16|G<<8|R.

3. Row pointers: pre-computar pSrcRow y pDstRow por fila.
   Elimina 3 multiplicaciones por pixel del inner loop.

Resultado: inner loop pasa de ~15 operaciones a ~8 por pixel.
A 800x600 @60fps = ~28M pixels/seg con menos overhead.

feat(NWA-225): migrate database layer from PostgreSQL to Cloudflare D1

Replace postgres-js driver with drizzle-orm/d1, rewrite all schemas from
pgTable to sqliteTable with appropriate type mappings (timestamp→integer,
boolean→integer), remove FOR UPDATE locks (unnecessary in D1 single-writer),
and update wrangler.jsonc to use d1_databases binding instead of Hyperdrive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore Vercel-compatible build output (dist/ not ../backend/static)

Vercel's build sandbox can't write outside the project directory, so
outDir: '../backend/static' was silently failing and Vercel kept serving
a stale cached build. Changed to outDir: 'dist' which Vercel auto-detects.

Also revert Railway buildCommand — Railway only runs the Python API server.
Vercel handles the frontend build independently with VITE_API_URL pointing
to the Railway backend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace 166 worker console calls with structured logger

Migrate all console.log/warn/error in worker/src/ to use the structured
logger from worker/src/logger.ts. Produces JSON-formatted log output
suitable for log aggregation. Only logger.ts itself retains console calls.

commit.message:
fix(kpi): sync match_result_json when LLM match finds new items

LLM match handler updated matched_items count but not the cached
match_result_json. This caused the KPI API/Table split to show
stale data after LLM matching. Now appends LLM matches to the
cached JSON and decrements unmatched counts accordingly.

commit.message:
Add CLI Login button — runs 'claude auth login' via Safari

Adds a terminal icon button in the popover footer that executes
claude auth login with BROWSER env set to a helper script that
forces Safari (bypasses default Chrome browser).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add Railway buildCommand to build frontend on every deploy

Previously Railway had no buildCommand so it never rebuilt the frontend
from source — it only served whatever static files were committed to git.
This caused stale UI even after JSX changes were pushed.

Now Railway runs `npm install && npm run build` in frontend-src on every
deploy, outputting fresh assets to backend/static/. No more manual
static file commits needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Remove legacy src directory

The monolithic Python application in src/ has been superseded by the
skills-based workflow in .claude/skills/. All functionality (video
processing, transcription, email generation) is now handled by
specialized skills.

Removed:
- src/agent.py (LangChain agent)
- src/cli.py (Click CLI)
- src/web_interface.py (Gradio UI)
- src/gmail_client.py (Google API client)
- src/drive_client.py (Google API client)
- src/summarizer.py (LLM logic)
- src/transcript_processor.py
- src/models.py
- src/config.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
